page: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It also includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are present.

Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core testing, image processing, and static web serving throughput. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth, reaching 254.01 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 109.18 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For database operations, the instance registers top-tier Passmark database scores and strong Redis SET performance at 83.04 million operations per second. Additionally, the Intel AMX instructions contribute to strong prompt processing speeds during LLM inference, particularly with large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64lds_v6 offers a high density of compute resources and local storage relative to its memory footprint. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory allocations, though it presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive databases. The combination of high-speed dedicated Intel Xeon cores, a large local disk, and advanced vector/matrix instructions makes this server well-suited for software compilation, image processing, web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 128.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, static web serving, image processing, and database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. The low-memory-to-CPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This server is highly suited for software compilation, web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v6 is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, fast local storage, and advanced matrix math acceleration.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8573C at 4.2 GHz
128 GB RAM providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix operations
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks and static web serving
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
